none: Nashville, University Center urban renewal area 1 : environmental impact statement.

The University Center Urban Renewal Area project in Nashville, Tennessee, aimed to revitalize a deteriorating area southwest of the city's central business district. The plan involved clearing and rehabilitating buildings to make way for the expansion of Vanderbilt University and its Medical Center. The project sought to improve the area's infrastructure, including transportation systems, and stabilize the residential community. Amendments to the project focused on land use changes and transportation improvements to reduce conflicts between pedestrians and vehicles.
Nashville, University Center urban renewal area 1 : environmental impact statement.

The University Center Urban Renewal Area project in Nashville, Tennessee, aimed to revitalize a deteriorating area southwest of the city's central business district. The plan involved clearing and rehabilitating buildings to make way for the expansion of Vanderbilt University and its Medical Center. The project sought to improve the area's infrastructure, including transportation systems, and stabilize the residential community. Amendments to the project focused on land use changes and transportation improvements to reduce conflicts between pedestrians and vehicles.
U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

The University Center Urban Renewal Area project was proposed to address the decline of a neighborhood in Nashville, aiming to enhance the area for both the community and Vanderbilt University. While there may have been some public concerns regarding displacement and changes to the neighborhood, the project ultimately sought to create a more functional and appealing environment. The initiative was part of a broader trend in urban renewal during the mid-20th century, focusing on revitalization and infrastructure improvements.
United States. Department of Housing and Urban Development

"Mr. Joseph Johnston, a project area resident, filed a complaint against the Nashville Metropolitan Development and Housing Agency and HUD which charged that the Department and the Housing Agency had not complied with the National Environmental Protection Act of 1969." — Public Comments – Section 5.2
